records.fact: content/site-content.ts is the main centralized source for navigation, marketing copy, card text, and route-level content objects.
The public production host is roofingrecyclers.com, and www should redirect to the apex domain.
records.source: Confirmed deployment and middleware setup during March 2026 work

records.fact: Lead submissions are validated in /api/leads and inserted into Supabase table public.inbound_leads using server-side service-role access.
Because tsconfig includes .next/types, an isolated npm run typecheck may fail before generated build artifacts exist; build first or rerun typecheck after build output is present.
records.source: Observed repeatedly during March 2026 verification work

records.fact: The site should feel industrial, premium, and investor-grade rather than like a local roofer site or a generic eco startup.
records.source: Confirmed user direction and existing site goals

records.fact: Public investor-facing copy should frame regulatory, policy, and manufacturer advantages carefully and avoid specific unverified claims.
records.source: Confirmed writing approach from investor-page work

records.fact: The home hero visual is sensitive to text density and should avoid nested narrow multi-column card layouts within the constrained right-hand hero column.
records.source: User feedback and follow-up hero visual fixes

records.fact: The route structure already anticipates future contractor, manufacturer, municipality, certification, policy, locations, and news expansion.
docs/project-memory is the canonical durable memory location for this repo and should be read and updated at the start and end of each future session.
The project memory system was committed and pushed to the repo beginning with commit 90d9cb3, so durable memory is no longer only local state.
records.source: Git commit and push performed on 2026-03-24

records.fact: The home hero reads best when the right-hand visual uses short overlay copy, one or two thesis blocks, and stacked operating rows instead of multi-column mini-cards with long paragraphs.
records.source: Direct user screenshot feedback and hero redesign on 2026-03-24

records.fact: Lead notifications are now implemented with Nodemailer/SMTP as a best-effort step after the Supabase insert; if SMTP credentials are missing, the form still succeeds and the lead remains stored in public.inbound_leads.

The default lead-notification recipient for this project is warephoto@yahoo.com, but actual delivery still requires SMTP_HOST, SMTP_PORT, SMTP_SECURE, SMTP_USER, SMTP_PASSWORD, and EMAIL_FROM to be configured.
records.source: User instruction and repo update on 2026-03-24

records.fact: Yahoo SMTP is the chosen delivery path for lead notifications. The local .env.local file is prefilled with Yahoo host, port, secure mode, sender, and recipient values; the remaining missing secret is the Yahoo app password.

A Yahoo SMTP credential provided on March 25, 2026 failed authentication with '535 5.7.0 (#AUTH005) Too many bad auth attempts'. SMTP_PASSWORD was removed from local and Vercel production settings afterward so the app would stop retrying invalid Yahoo logins.
records.source: Direct SMTP verification and production cleanup on 2026-03-25

records.fact: A second supplied Yahoo credential also failed local SMTP verification on March 25, 2026 with the same 535 / AUTH005 error. It was not written into local or production config. Inference: the project likely needs an actual Yahoo app password rather than another account-style password.

A Yahoo app password verified successfully on March 25, 2026. SMTP_PASSWORD is now restored in local env and Vercel production, the live site was redeployed, a direct SMTP send succeeded, and a production form submission succeeded with SMTP active.
records.source: Direct SMTP verification, Vercel deployment, and live submission test on 2026-03-25
